title: Docker hangs waiting for podman daemon to tell it to die
body:
### Issue Description

This issue is kinda subtle and I don't fully understand it. I ran into it on an Amazon Linux 2023 server where I use podman daemon and docker client from Nixpkgs. Podman works normally for the login user, but for a system user that I created, even in interactive sessions I start via `sudo -u some-user -i`, `docker run hello-world` hangs.

There aren't any interesting configuration differences I could find. Using `docker run --rm` avoids the hang.

I understand vaguely that it has to do with running things as a system user who doesn't have normal systemd-logind treatment or something.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

I asked Claude Code to write me a little reproducer script, and I've tested it in a fresh Fedora 43 VM via Lima. Please just use it in your own Fedora 43 VM; that's the easiest way. I've attached it here. The reproducer script does need root. Please run it anyway (it's short and easy to read).

If you want to play interactively:

1. Set up a Fedora 43 VM.
2. Run the reproducer script once (as root).
3. Log in as `podman-hang-test` with `sudo -u podman-hang-test -i`
4. Run some `docker run` commands, with and without `--rm`, like `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/run/user/$(id -u podman-hang-test) DOCKER_HOST=unix:///run/user/$(id -u podman-hang-test)/podman/podman.sock docker run --rm docker.io/hello-world`
5. note that Docker is hung :)

on Docker 25 this is more painful because the signal proxy stops `^C` from working and you have to kill it from outside. On Docker 29 it's not so bad; just hit `^C` three times.

[reproduce.sh](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26733045/reproduce.sh)


### Describe the results you received

The thing hangs! You should see output like mine:

```
[patcal04@lima-podman-repro podman-compat-wait-hang]$ sudo bash reproduce.sh 
=== Podman compat API hang reproducer ===
kernel: 6.17.1-300.fc43.aarch64

Installing podman + docker CLI...
Updating and loading repositories:
Repositories loaded.
Package "podman-5:5.6.2-1.fc43.aarch64" is already installed.

Nothing to do.
File "/etc/yum.repos.d/docker-ce.repo" already exists and configures repositories with IDs "docker-ce-stable docker-ce-stable-source docker-ce-test docker-ce-test-source". Add "--overwrite" to overwrite.
Updating and loading repositories:
Repositories loaded.
Package "docker-ce-cli-1:29.4.0-1.fc43.aarch64" is already installed.

Nothing to do.
podman: podman version 5.6.2
docker: Docker version 29.4.0, build 9d7ad9f

--- Setting up system user with rootless podman ---
useradd: user 'podman-hang-test' already exists
Podman socket: /run/user/501/podman/podman.sock

--- docker run (hangs if bug is present) ---
If this hangs after printing hello-world output, the bug is confirmed.
Will be killed after 30s.


Hello from Docker!
This message shows that your installation appears to be working correctly.

To generate this message, Docker took the following steps:
 1. The Docker client contacted the Docker daemon.
 2. The Docker daemon pulled the "hello-world" image from the Docker Hub.
    (arm64v8)
 3. The Docker daemon created a new container from that image which runs the
    executable that produces the output you are currently reading.
 4. The Docker daemon streamed that output to the Docker client, which sent it
    to your terminal.

To try something more ambitious, you can run an Ubuntu container with:
 $ docker run -it ubuntu bash

Share images, automate workflows, and more with a free Docker ID:
 https://hub.docker.com/

For more examples and ideas, visit:
 https://docs.docker.com/get-started/

reproduce.sh: line 75:  5079 Killed                     timeout --kill-after=5 30 sudo -u "$TESTUSER" XDG_RUNTIME_DIR="$RUN_DIR" DOCKER_HOST="unix://$SOCK" docker run docker.io/hello-world

Cleanup: sudo bash reproduce.sh --cleanup
[patcal04@lima-podman-repro podman-compat-wait-hang]$ 

```

### Describe the results you expected

I should get my terminal back when the container dies

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

I'm sorry I couldn't give a better bug report than this, but it's at least reproducible via that script on Fedora 43. Feel free to tweak it as desired. Please lmk what you discover

comments.body: I attached podman info output from two systems there, sorry if that's a problem. Also I realize I brain-farted on some things I wrote because I was interrupted mid-sentence. Please read the edited issue instead of just your first notification email. Sorry!

i dont see any attachments and also you should provide output from the podman system service if it reveals something.  consider running the service in debug mode ... `podman --log-level=debug systems service -t0`

I was only able to reproduce the issue using the latest Podman service and Docker client, and it requires a freshly booted machine. Because the container exits before event logging initializes, the issue occurs when the `/wait` event subscription happens after the container has already terminated. I believe I know what the problem is and will work on a fix.
